entry.title: Positioning SAP Customer Loyalty Management: Turning Loyalty into a Growth Engine
entry.content:
In today’s hyper-competitive digital landscape, customer loyalty is no longer just about points and rewards—it’s about building meaningful, personalized relationships that drive long-term value. This is where SAP Customer Loyalty Management stands out as a powerful solution within the SAP Customer Experience portfolio.

What is SAP Customer Loyalty Management?

SAP Customer Loyalty Management is a cloud-native, SaaS solution designed to help organizations create, manage, and scale loyalty programs across multiple brands, markets, and channels.

Unlike traditional loyalty systems, it acts as a central hub for customer engagement, enabling businesses to transform loyalty programs from cost centers into revenue-generating strategic assets.

At its core, the solution empowers companies to:

  • Deliver personalized, data-driven experiences
  • Manage loyalty programs globally from a single platform
  • Build stronger emotional connections with customers
  • Turn customers into long-term brand advocates

Why Positioning Matters

Positioning SAP Customer Loyalty Management is not about listing features—it’s about aligning the solution with real business challenges.

Modern businesses face:

  • Fragmented customer data
  • Low engagement and retention
  • Difficulty proving ROI from loyalty initiatives

SAP addresses these challenges by reframing loyalty as a strategic growth driver, not just a marketing tactic by:

  • Centralizing loyalty programs across brands and markets
  • Delivering personalized, omnichannel experiences
  • Providing a unified customer “wallet” for seamless engagement
  • Tracking ROI to prove real business impact

The shift:

Loyalty is no longer a cost center—it’s a revenue-generating engine that boosts retention, engagement, and customer lifetime value.

Bottom line:

With SAP, companies don’t just manage loyalty—they build meaningful customer relationships that drive growth.

Our Course featuring SAP Customer Loyalty Management:

Positioning SAP Customer Loyalty Management

entry.title: Introducing Performance Analyzer in SAP HANA Cloud Central
entry.content:
Overview 

SAP HANA Cloud Central now includes the Performance Analyzer, a new capability for visualizing and analyzing historical performance data of SAP HANA Cloud database instances. This feature enables administrators to identify performance issues, investigate root causes, and make data-driven decisions based on up to 42 days of historical metrics. 

This blog post provides an overview of the Performance Analyzer, its key features, and guidance on how to use it effectively. 

Background 

Performance troubleshooting in cloud database environments requires visibility into historical data. While real-time monitoring provides insight into current system state, identifying the performance degradation or correlating issues with specific events requires access to historical metrics. 

The Performance Analyzer addresses this need by providing comprehensive historical data visualization, interactive exploration capabilities, and customizable views for different analysis scenarios. 

Key Features 

Load History Chart 

The Load History chart serves as the primary navigation tool within the Performance Analyzer. It displays up to 42 days of historical data for selected key performance indicators (KPIs), enabling administrators to: 

  • Identify time periods with anomalous behavior 
  • Select specific time ranges for detailed analysis 
  • Correlate performance changes with system events 

The chart includes two sections: an overview panel showing the full 42-day history, and a detail panel that displays granular data for the selected time range. 

Chart Library 

The Performance Analyzer includes a Chart Library containing visualization components organized by category: 

  • CPU, Memory, Lock, Transaction, Workload, Delta merge, Statement, Session, Savepoint 

Administrators can browse charts by category, search for specific visualizations, and preview charts before adding them to the current view. 

Preset and Custom Views 

The Performance Analyzer provides preset views optimized for common analysis scenarios: 

CPU: Charts relevant to processor utilization and statement performance 

Memory: Charts focused on memory consumption patterns 

Lock: Charts for transactional lock and concurrency analysis 

Administrators can also create custom views by modifying preset configurations, adding charts from the library, and saving personalized layouts for future use. 

Interactive Exploration 

All charts within the Performance Analyzer support interactive data exploration: 

  • Tooltips display precise values when hovering over data points 
  • Time range selections synchronize across all charts in the view 
  • Zoom and pan controls enable detailed examination of specific periods 

Accessing the Performance Analyzer 

The Performance Analyzer is accessible through multiple entry points within SAP HANA Cloud Central: 

Side Navigation 

Select the Performance Analyzer icon in the left navigation panel to access the tool directly. 

Instance Overview 

From the instance Overview page, navigate to the Memory or Compute cards and select "Analyze Performance." 

Alerts Integration 

When viewing alerts related to CPU, memory, or transactional locks, select "Analyze Performance Issue" in the alert details to open the Performance Analyzer with the relevant time context. 

Command Palette 

Open the Command Palette, search for "Performance Analyzer," and select the target instance from the results. 

Joule Integration 

The Performance Analyzer is also accessible through Joule. Request performance analysis or enter "Performance Analyzer" to receive navigation assistance. 

Use Cases 

Analyzing CPU Utilization 

To investigate CPU-related performance issues: 

  1. Access the Performance Analyzer and select the CPU preset view 
  2. Use the Load History chart to identify periods of elevated CPU usage 
  3. Select the time range of interest to view detailed metrics 
  4. Add relevant charts such as "Total Execution Time by Statement Hash" 

Analyze which statements or operations contributed to CPU consumption 

Investigating Memory Consumption 

To examine memory usage patterns: 

  1. Select the Memory preset view 
  2. Review the Load History chart for unusual consumption trends 
  3. Add memory allocator charts to identify contributing components 

Examine host allocator visualizations to determine memory distribution 

Troubleshooting Performance Degradation 

To investigate general performance degradation: 

  1. Use the Load History chart to establish baseline performance patterns 
  2. Identify the time when degradation began 
  3. Compare metrics from before and during the performance issue 

Add relevant charts to analyze changes in system behavior 

Summary 

The Performance Analyzer in SAP HANA Cloud Central provides administrators with comprehensive tools for historical performance analysis. By combining extensive data retention, flexible visualization options, and multiple access methods, it enables efficient identification and resolution of performance issues in SAP HANA Cloud environments.

entry.title: The AI Revolution in CX: How SAP Business AI is Redefining the Customer Journey
entry.content:
Today’s customers demand intelligent, personalized experiences. To deliver, businesses need more than just good intentions—they need powerful, embedded intelligence. SAP is delivering this with SAP Business AI, a comprehensive strategy that infuses the entire Customer Experience (CX) suite with practical, powerful artificial intelligence, fronted by its conversational copilot, Joule.

This isn't just about AI for your customers; it's about AI for your entire organization.

 AI for Your Frontline Teams: Smarter Engagement

SAP Business AI works out-of-the-box to make your daily operations more intelligent. With Joule as their copilot, your teams can work faster and smarter.

  • For Sales: Joule drafts personalized emails and summarizes opportunities, while embedded AI scores leads so reps can focus on the deals most likely to close.
  • For Service: Joule instantly summarizes complex customer tickets, and AI recommends the best knowledge base solutions, slashing resolution times and boosting satisfaction.
  • For Commerce: AI-powered search and product recommendations create a bespoke shopping experience for every single visitor, driving conversion and loyalty.

AI for Your Project Teams: Faster Implementations

SAP’s AI vision goes deeper, empowering the people who build your CX solutions with Joule for Consultants (J4C).

Think of J4C as an expert "AI librarian" for your project team. It’s a specialized copilot that provides instant, conversational access to SAP's vast knowledge base. Consultants can ask for best practices, configuration guidance, and even code intelligence. By mastering smart prompting, teams can dramatically reduce project complexity and accelerate delivery with confidence.

Ready to Master SAP Business AI?

Dive deep by enrolling in the official SAP Learning course and earning your Badge!

Introducing SAP Business AI for SAP Customer Experience

entry.title: Re: ACL is not considered in PS-IS reporting in S/4HANA!
entry.content:
Hello, ACLs have been modernized by provisioning the ability to read ACLs based on CDS views, so, as of SAP S/4HANA 2021, with SAP Note 3641266 then ACLs are no longer part of the compatibility scope. That means you can use it again. But ACL function will not be updated anymore. 

From SAP S/4HANA 2025, SAP recommends transitioning to object-based authorization in project system. You can find more information about the object-based authorization in SAP Note 3646104.

entry.title: CBC behavior when an activated scope item is deprecated or removed
entry.content:
In SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC), I have a question about SAP upgrades where the scope item itself becomes deprecated or planned for removal, not cases where apps or features used within a scope item are deprecated.

If a scope item that is already activated in CBC becomes deprecated or scheduled for removal, and a successor scope item is introduced:

  • Will the successor scope item be automatically activated during the upgrade?
  • Will it be automatically applied or transported to the customer systems (e.g. test / production)?
  • What happens to the original scope item that is deprecated or removed?
    • Is it automatically deactivated or deleted during the upgrade?

Or does this scenario require manual scope adjustment and migration activities by the customer or implementation team?

entry.title: Re: Questiosn regarding onPrem systems
entry.content:
According to the KBA 2459283 - How to link customer numbers using the Corporate Group function (CCC) - SAP for Me , adding a subsidiary to a CCC group requires the CCC group's Super admin to open a case. Therefore, please contact the Super admin listed below to open a new case on your behalf. 
After linking * to the CCC group, please also ask the Super admin to assign the appropriate authorizations to the S-users under *.

Relevant authorizations:

  • Report Technical Problem on CCC level
  • Send Cases to SAP on CCC level
  • Display Cases on CCC level
  • Display All Cases on Global Level
  • Close Cases on CCC level
  • Display cloud data（only apply to cloud installations）

entry.title: Question about API-based depreciation expense allocation for fixed assets across multiple company co
entry.content:
Current Situation

We are investigating whether it is possible to execute depreciation expense allocation for fixed assets via API for multiple company codes in parallel.

At the moment, our processing is executed sequentially in the following order:

  • Company A data → API processing completed
  • Company B data → API processing completed

The customer is asking whether the process can be changed so that the data for Company A and Company B can be processed at the same time in parallel, in order to reduce processing time.

Detailed Background

Our understanding is as follows:

  • The target business process is depreciation expense allocation for company fixed assets.
  • The customer would like to improve performance by processing multiple company codes simultaneously.
  • The question is not only about general batch processing, but specifically about whether parallel execution is supported or safe when the same fixed asset may be involved.
  • We would like to understand whether SAP has any official guidance, best practice, limitation, or known behavior for this kind of scenario.

Questions

  1. Is it technically supported to execute depreciation expense allocation for fixed assets via API for multiple company codes in parallel?
  2. If parallel execution is possible, are there any official SAP documents, API guides, best practices, or restrictions that describe this behavior?
  3. If multiple parallel API requests update the same fixed asset at nearly the same time, can this cause:
    • lock conflicts,
    • update conflicts,
    • or failed updates?
  4. If anyone has implemented a similar process, could you please share:
    • the API/service used,
    • whether parallel execution worked,
    • any issues encountered,
    • and how those issues were avoided?

Thank you very much for your support.

entry.title: Re: Moving Average Price is not getting updated
entry.content: If your goal is for the Purchase Order price to be the source of truth for the material's value at the time of receipt, Price Control V is the only way to achieve this without manual pre-work. Price Control S is designed specifically to ignore the PO price in favor of a pre-calculated standard, which is logically impossible for a batch that is being born during the GRN.

entry.title: Re: SAP System Changes Required – Transition from Income-...
entry.content:
Wait for the official note by SAP for ECC.

As per the new income tax act 2025, there is a change in the nomenclature of withholding office key. i.e., for e.g., TDS section 194C will be known as 393(1) [Table: S.No. 6(a)] , 393(1) [Table: S.No. 6(b)]  along with new section codes like 1023/1024 representing 194C. These changes in official keys will have implications in TDS/TCS returns. Hence, mapping of old withholding keys with new withholding keys will suffice the requirement in SAP as far as withholding tax changes are concern.

So, instead of creating new keys and associating these new keys with existing tax types & codes, I recommend waiting for note by SAP providing the above mapping, which will suffice your requirement.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Deprecation and The Path Forward
entry.content:

My main question is: moving forward, are low-code development platforms like this going to be entirely replaced by GenAI/GenUI? Or have I misunderstood the future direction?

Best Wishes,

Jack

entry.title: How to Prepare an Indirect Method Cash Flow Statement in Compliance with Chinese Accounting Standard
entry.content:
I need to prepare a cash flow statement under Chinese Accounting Standards (CAS). There is an issue with the system’s custom analytical queries, so I found the app Cash Flow Statement - Indirect Method. I created a new financial statement version (FSV) to be used for the indirect cash flow statement. However, after selecting this financial statement version and executing the report in the Cash Flow Statement - Indirect Method app, the values in the measure columns are not related to the items in my financial statement version. The measures generated by this app do not match the content of the indirect method under Chinese Accounting Standards.
 
In addition, I have not assigned semantic tags to the financial statement version, because the semantic tags for this app shown in SAP Learning also do not comply with the requirements of Chinese Accounting Standards.
 
(Reference: https://learning.sap.com/courses/financial-statement-reporting-zh/creating-the-cash-flow-statement-indirect-method_bf92692d-7d4d-47d4-93a0-47641afc4485)

entry.title: Re: Automatic G/L Account Determination Based on Material...
entry.content:
Hello @easap ,


There is no standard configuration to determine G/L directly from Material Type
A)Use valuation class mapping (standard solution)
B)If strict requirement → use BAdI MB_ACCOUNTING_BADI


A)Recommended Solution (Best Practice)


-Define valuation classes per material type
-Assign valuation class in material master
-Maintain OBYC accordingly
This achieves indirect Material Type → G/L mapping

B)Enhancement Approach (Only if unavoidable)

BAdI Implementation
BAdI: MB_ACCOUNTING_BADI

Logic flow:
Read Material Type (MARA-MTART)
Map to desired G/L account
Override account before posting

Summary:-

✔ SAP best practice = Material Type → Valuation Class → G/L Account
❌ Avoid direct Material Type → G/L unless absolutely required

 

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: fg order start & end date consider production start a...
entry.content: In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, the system meets your requirement through multi-level BOM scheduling combined with routing and lead-time offset logic. The standard approach is to use production versions, routings, and MRP scheduling so that the start/end dates of finished goods are automatically calculated based on the dependent requirements of their multi-level components. Direct “component-level date inheritance” is not configurable at the BOM level, but the system achieves this via MRP and order scheduling.

entry.title: Step-by-step implementation guide for SAP S/4HANA Event Mesh (S4 as Producer, CPI as Consumer)
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I’m currently exploring the implementation of SAP Event Mesh (not AEM) with S/4HANA as the event producer and SAP Integration Suite (CPI) as the consumer.

While there are several SAP blogs and documentation references explaining Event Mesh concepts and architecture, I have not been able to find a single end-to-end guide that provides clear step-by-step instructions for implementing the following scenario:

Scenario:
S/4HANA → Event Mesh → SAP CPI (Integration Suite)

Specifically, I am looking for guidance on:

  1. Configuration steps in S/4HANA to enable event publishing (Event Enablement / Enterprise Eventing).

  2. Setting up SAP Event Mesh (queues, topics, service keys, etc.).

  3. Connecting SAP CPI as an event consumer (AMQP / webhook / queue subscription).

  4. Best practices for event topic design and queue subscriptions.

  5. Any recommended patterns for downstream integration (e.g., CPI → S/4 or CPI → third-party systems).

If anyone has implemented this pattern or can share:

  • a step-by-step guide

  • architecture recommendations

  • SAP documentation or reference implementations

it would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: CAPEX BUDGET PR/PO LEVEL PUBLIC CLOUD
entry.content:
Below is the standard approaches in Public Cloud

1) Use WBS Elements for CAPEX
Create a CAPEX project with WBS elements. Assign budgets at WBS level. Link PR/PO to the WBS (account assignment P). Budget check happens automatically during PR/PO creation and posting.

2) Settlement to Asset Under Construction (AUC)
Costs collected on the WBS are later settled to an AUC asset. Once the project is complete, settlement moves values from AUC to the final fixed asset. This way, you control CAPEX spending via WBS budgets, while still capitalizing to assets.

3) Budget Reporting
Use apps like Monitor Projects or Project Control – Financials to track budget vs. actuals. The system shows consumption at WBS level, not asset class level.

entry.title: Re: Sales org. filter condition for Billing Document Requ...
entry.content: The system cannot read billable items when you filter only by sales org because it requires the full sales area combination. Make sure the service confirmation and customizing have consistent sales org + distribution channel + division assignments, and use that full set in your BDR selection.

entry.title: Re: Evaluated Receipt Settlement - manual invoice posting...
entry.content: In Public Cloud, there is no fully manual way to create an ERS invoice outside the scheduled job. Manual invoices cannot be flagged as ERS, so they won’t meet your compliance needs. The only viable path is to continue using the ERS job and raise a requirement with SAP to expose the Tax Reporting Date field for adjustment.

entry.title: Re: Isn't RAP overengineered ?
entry.content:
Whilst I do agree in the form that RAP is overwhelming at first and yes it can take more time than a classic report depending on what is required for the report.

I also see the positive side of RAP.
It does not only give you the ability to generate a fiori application on top of the service, it also provides you with the services that allow access to the information and operations (actions) of the report for external parties without extra effort.
Besides that it can also go much further than a simple report in the sense that it can become a complex application with drilldowns to as many lower object pages with tables as I personally ever required and the option to have actions (specific logic) for each of them. 
It also supports draft handling and even collaborative draft handling.
And perhaps the main reason of its existence, it is part of the clean core strategy.

Once you get passed the overwhelming part and into the nice things of RAP you will likely start appreciating it

As a wise man once said "There is nothing so useless as doing efficiently that which should not be done at all".

I hope you may come to like RAP eventually.

entry.title: Re: Sales return to a consignmnet supplier stock
entry.content: To return goods into supplier consignment stock, you must use the consignment return movement types (651K/653K) in your return delivery. If your system is defaulting to own stock, adjust the item category/movement type determination so that the “K” variant is used.

entry.title: Re: Public cloud: can we have reaction for credit check w...
entry.content:
Good day @nauttawanit 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, day based overdue checks in Credit Management can be achieved, but the exact staged reaction you described (30 days overdue = warning only and 60 days overdue = warning and block) is not supported in one single automatic credit check setup.

What you can achieve in Credit Management is:

  • Check step 110: Age of Oldest Open Item
  • Check step 130: Overdue Open Items

See the following references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/56ebef06e051488b97fd0faddd3c8837.html?locale=en-US

3016662 - Credit check not successful Error message: Percentage 100% of overdue open items exceeds 0% ( days) - SAP for Me

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/4d4653b0cfd70692e10000000a42189b.html?locale=en-US

You can configure your system to check overdue behaviour by number of days. For example:

  1. Check whether the oldest open item exceeds a defined number of days
  2. Check whether the percentage of overdue open items exceeds a threshold after a given number of days

Instead of trying the approach you mentioned, you may consider the following:

  • Choose one standard reaction for the rule, where your system warns once overdue threshold is exceeded, or error/block once the overdue threshold is exceeded.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/b658136d71c941ebb629230b2b8f09e0.html?locale=en-US

  • Option B is for you to use different checking rules for different scenarios. See KBA 2967721 for more details.
  • Alternatively (option C), you may consider creating a manual follow up after warning, where you configure the credit rule with warning, monitor payment behaviour in Manage Credit Accounts and then apply manual review or documented credit decision handling for stronger intervention. Note that these do not create an automatic two stage warning then block sequence, but it is a clean standard process.

 

The following references provide guidance on Credit Management Calculation.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/be5747120f22446dbaeabc02044722d3.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/32c724ee32d54e3eb43004e2844b124d.html?locale=en-US

To summarise:

you would normally choose either a warning-based rule or a blocking rule for the defined overdue threshold. If your business requires a two-stage escalation, you will need to handle that through a process design or an enhancement approach (non-standard).

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: ABAP Developer Challenge - Joule for Developers using Basic Trial
entry.content:
Package: ZAC_1555968

Week 4 Challenge has been completed😀.

- Used determination and Joule Code Prediction Capability.





- provided prompt as below

With some minor adjustments( instead of sy-datum , we use cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( ) class method)

 METHOD get_brew_date.

    READ ENTITIES OF zr_brew_am IN LOCAL MODE
         ENTITY brew
         FIELDS ( brewdate )
         WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
         RESULT DATA(entities).

    DATA update_table TYPE TABLE FOR UPDATE zr_brew_am.

    LOOP AT entities ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<entity>).
      APPEND VALUE #( %tky = <entity>-%tky
                      brewdate = cl_abap_context_info=>get_system_date( ) - 7 ) TO update_table.
    ENDLOOP.

    MODIFY ENTITIES OF zr_brew_am IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY brew
        UPDATE FIELDS ( brewdate )
        WITH CORRESPONDING #( update_table ).
  ENDMETHOD.



- The week 4 task was accomplished. 
Determine Brew Date using determination and Joule's Code Prediction Capability



Brew Date is current date - 7 days 😋

 

Happy Learning and Jouling 😊!!

entry.title: Re: How do l implement a user exit or badi to retrigger r...
entry.content:
Hello @THenry ,

from the tagged solutions i am not quite sure which system you are working on.

For S/4HANA Public Cloud you could also look into Customizing Activity 103345 Manage Conditions to Restart Flexible Workflow for Purchase Orders.

 

Please see here a copy of the activity's description:

In this configuration step, you can select various attributes for purchase orders that are relevant for the restart of the flexible workflow. Whenever you update the values of these attributes in the purchase orders, the system triggers a restart of the approval process. 

 

Here is an example of how that could be set up:

 

→ To apply that to your specific question "retrigger PO release strategy when there is a net price, quantity or net value increase or decrease":

All three changes of net price, quantity or net value will result in an update of the total net order value, so setting up the attribute GNETW Total Net Order Value would be sufficient here.

 

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: KPI (Key Performance Indicator): A Simple Guide
entry.content:
Introduction:  A Key Performance Indicator (KPI) is a measurable value used to evaluate how effectively a business or organization is achieving its key objectives. In SAP Fiori Elements, KPIs are used to display important business metrics in a clear and visual format. They help users quickly monitor performance, identify trends, and make data-driven decisions.

KPI (Key Performance Indicator):  It is a measurable value used to evaluate how effectively a business or organization is achieving its objectives. KPIs help businesses monitor performance, identify trends, and support data-driven decision-making.

In modern enterprise applications built with SAP Fiori Elements, KPIs provide a quick overview of key business metrics directly within dashboards and analytical applications.

KPI in Analytical List Page:

The Analytical List Page (ALP) is a powerful Fiori Elements template that combines:

  • KPIs
  • Charts
  • Filters
  • Tables

KPIs are displayed at the Top of the page and help users quickly analyze business data.

Note: In this article, I have not covered the creation of the CDS View, OData service exposure, or the application creation in SAP BTP, as these topics were already explained in my previous blogs. In this post, I focus only on providing an example of the KPI annotations and explaining where these annotations are added in the project.

CDS:

You can add the KPI annotations in the Metadata Extension file. This approach helps keep UI-related annotations separate from the core CDS view and allows developers to manage UI configurations more efficiently.

KPI Annotation:

.chart: [{
    qualifier: 'chartDefault',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #PIE,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    },
    {
    qualifier: 'kpidata',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #DONUT,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    }

   ]

@UI.presentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'Default',
    visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART ,qualifier: 'chartDefault'}]
    },
 {
 qualifier: 'KPIAMT',text: 'KPI: Amount by Year',
 visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART,qualifier: 'kpidata'},{ type: #AS_DATAPOINT,qualifier: 'Amount'
}]

}]

@UI.selectionVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    text: 'Amount by Year'
}]
@UI.selectionPresentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    presentationVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    selectionVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT'
}]

Once the annotations are added in the Metadata Extension, you can configure the KPI annotation in the manifest.json file so that it is recognized and displayed in the Analytical List Page application.

qualifier: 'KPIAMT'
 

"keyPerformanceIndicators": {
                            "kpiNayak": {
                                "model": "KPI1",
                                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                                "qualifier": "KPIAMT"
                            }
                        }

Path:

sap.ui.generic.app → pages → AnalyticalListPage → component → settings → keyPerformanceIndicators
 "pages": {
            "AnalyticalListPage|Ztest_ALP_Report": {
                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                "component": {
                    "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.AnalyticalListPage",
                    "list": true,
                    "settings": {
                        "condensedTableLayout": true,
                        "showGoButtonOnFilterBar": true,
                        "qualifier": "Default",
                        "autoHide": true,
                        "smartVariantManagement": false,
                        "tableSettings": {
                            "multiSelect": false,
                            "type": "AnalyticalTable"
                        },
                        "keyPerformanceIndicators": {
                            "kpiNayak": {
                                "model": "KPI1",
                                "entitySet": "Ztest_ALP_Report",
                                "qualifier": "KPIAMT"
                            }
                        }
                    }
                },

Note : After that, ensure that the model used for the KPI configuration is also created under the models section in the manifest.json file. The same model name defined for the KPI must be maintained in the models configuration so that the application can correctly retrieve the KPI data from the OData service.

The model name used in the KPI configuration (for example, KPI1) must also be created under the models section in the manifest.json file. The same model name should be maintained .

Path:

sap.ui5 → models → KPI1

    "KPI1": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false
                }
            },
"models": {
            "i18n": {
                "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
                "settings": {
                    "bundleName": "analyticalreport.i18n.i18n"
                }
            },
            "": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false,
                    "metadataUrlParams": {
                        "sap-value-list": "none"
                    }
                }
            },
            "KPI1": {
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "preload": true,
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
                    "refreshAfterChange": false
                }
            },

Once all the configurations are completed, the KPI is successfully created. You can see the KPI displayed in the Analytical List Page as shown in the screenshot below for your reference.

Full Codes: This code example covers the Analytical List Page, including the configuration of Visual Filters and KPI annotations used in the application.

1.CDS View:
-------------
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZALPR'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'ALP Report'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define view Ztest_ALP_Report
  as select from ztest_sales_tab
{
  key vbeln as SalesDocument,
      vkorg as SalesOrg,
      werks as Plant,
      vtweg as DistrChannel,
      @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
      netwr as Amount,
      waerk as Currency,
      audat as D_Date,
      case substring( audat, 5, 2 )
      when '01' then concat( 'JAN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '02' then concat( 'FEB-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '03' then concat( 'MAR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '04' then concat( 'APR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '05' then concat( 'MAY-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '06' then concat( 'JUN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '07' then concat( 'JUL-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '08' then concat( 'AUG-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '09' then concat( 'SEP-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '10' then concat( 'OCT-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '11' then concat( 'NOV-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '12' then concat( 'DEC-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )

      end   as Month_Year
}
___________________________________
2.Meta data Extension:
------------------------
@Metadata.layer: #CORE

@UI.chart: [{
    qualifier: 'chartDefault',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #PIE,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    },
    {
    qualifier: 'kpidata',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #DONUT,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    }

   ]

@UI.presentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'Default',
    visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART ,qualifier: 'chartDefault'}]
    },
 {
 qualifier: 'KPIAMT',text: 'KPI: Amount by Year',
 visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART,qualifier: 'kpidata'},{ type: #AS_DATAPOINT,qualifier: 'Amount'
}]

}]

@UI.selectionVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    text: 'Amount by Year'
}]
@UI.selectionPresentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    presentationVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT',
    selectionVariantQualifier: 'KPIAMT'
}]


annotate entity Ztest_ALP_Report with
{
  .lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 10 }]

  SalesDocument;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 20 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 20 }]
  SalesOrg;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 30 }]
  Plant;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 40 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 40 }]
  DistrChannel;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 50 }]
  .dataPoint.title: 'Total Amount'
  .dataPoint.qualifier: 'Amount'
  Amount;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 60 }]
  Currency;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 70 }]
  D_Date;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 80 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 80 }]
  Month_Year;

}
__________________________________
3. Annotation.xml file:
-----------------------------
<edmx:Edmx xmlns:edmx="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edmx" Version="4.0">
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/Common.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.Common.v1" Alias="Common"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="https://sap.github.io/odata-vocabularies/vocabularies/UI.xml">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1" Alias="UI"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:Reference Uri="/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV/$metadata">
        <edmx:Include Namespace="ZTEST_PURCHASE_DATA_SRV" Alias="SAP"/>
    </edmx:Reference>
    <edmx:DataServices>
        <Schema xmlns="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edm" Namespace="local">
           
            <Annotations Target="SAP.Ztest_ALP_ReportType">
                <Annotation Term="UI.Chart" Qualifier="Step1_CQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.ChartDefinitionType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="ChartType" EnumMember="UI.ChartType/Bar"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Title" String="Purchase Order Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Description" String="Purchase Order Data"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Dimensions">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Month_Year</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="DimensionAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartDimensionAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Dimension" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartDimensionRoleType/Category"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Measures">
                            <Collection>
                                <PropertyPath>Amount</PropertyPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                        <PropertyValue Property="MeasureAttributes">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="UI.ChartMeasureAttributeType">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Measure" PropertyPath="Amount"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="Role" EnumMember="UI.ChartMeasureRoleType/Axis1"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
                <Annotation Term="UI.PresentationVariant" Qualifier="Step2_pQ">
                    <Record Type="UI.PresentationVariantType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="Visualizations">
                            <Collection>
                                <AnnotationPath>@UI.Chart#Step1_CQ</AnnotationPath>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>
            <Annotations Target="SAP.Ztest_ALP_ReportType/Month_Year">
                <Annotation Term="Common.ValueList">
                    <Record Type="Common.ValueListType">
                        <PropertyValue Property="CollectionPath" String="Ztest_ALP_Report"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="PresentationVariantQualifier" String="Step2_pQ"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="Parameters">
                            <Collection>
                                <Record Type="Common.ValueListParameterInOut">
                                    <PropertyValue Property="LocalDataProperty" PropertyPath="Month_Year"/>
                                    <PropertyValue Property="ValueListProperty" String="Month_Year"/>
                                </Record>
                            </Collection>
                        </PropertyValue>
                    </Record>
                </Annotation>
            </Annotations>
           
           
        </Schema>
    </edmx:DataServices>
</edmx:Edmx>
 _____________________________

entry.title: How to Create an Analytical List Page in SAP Fiori Elements – Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
Introduction : The Analytical List Page (ALP) in SAP Fiori Elements is a powerful floorplan designed for data analysis and monitoring business performance. It combines visual charts, visual filters, and a data table within a single page, allowing users to analyze large amounts of data efficiently. The Analytical List Page enables users to quickly identify trends, filter information, and drill down into detailed data for better business insights.

Analytical List Page (ALP) in SAP Fiori Elements:

The Analytical List Page (ALP) is a SAP Fiori Elements floorplan designed for data analysis and reporting. It combines charts, filters, and tables in a single page so users can analyze business data interactively.

ALP allows users to visualize, filter, and drill down into data without creating custom UI code.

Key Features of Analytical List Page:

  1.  Visual Filter--->The filter bar allows users to filter data using graphical representations.
  2. Analytical Charts--->Charts provide a visual representation of aggregated business data.
  3. Data Table--->The table shows detailed records corresponding to the chart data.
  4. Interactive Filtering--->When a user clicks a chart segment, the table automatically filters the related records.

Flow of  Analytical List Page:

  1. CDS View
  2. Annotation
  3. Odata service
  4. SAP Fiori Elements ( Analytical List Page) Template
  5. SAP Fiori Application UI

Step 1: Create CDS View and Metadata Extension

First, create the Core Data Services (CDS) view that exposes the required business data.
After creating the CDS view, create a Metadata Extension and add the necessary UI annotations to define how the data should appear on the SAP Fiori Elements Analytical List Page.

The metadata extension is used to separate the UI annotations from the main CDS view, which helps maintain a cleaner and more structured development approach.

Note: It is assumed that you already know how to create a CDS View and a Metadata Extension in the ABAP environment. Therefore, this article does not cover the step-by-step creation process. Instead, the examples below directly demonstrate the CDS view and Metadata Extension with the required annotations used for the SAP Fiori Elements Object Page.

1. CDS View:

Code:

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZALPR'
@AbapCatalog.compiler.compareFilter: true
@AbapCatalog.preserveKey: true
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'ALP Report'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
define view Ztest_ALP_Report
  as select from ztest_sales_tab
{
  key vbeln as SalesDocument,
      vkorg as SalesOrg,
      werks as Plant,
      vtweg as DistrChannel,
      @DefaultAggregation: #SUM
      netwr as Amount,
      waerk as Currency,
      audat as D_Date,
      case substring( audat, 5, 2 )
      when '01' then concat( 'JAN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '02' then concat( 'FEB-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '03' then concat( 'MAR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '04' then concat( 'APR-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '05' then concat( 'MAY-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '06' then concat( 'JUN-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '07' then concat( 'JUL-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '08' then concat( 'AUG-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '09' then concat( 'SEP-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '10' then concat( 'OCT-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '11' then concat( 'NOV-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )
      when '12' then concat( 'DEC-', substring( audat, 1, 4 ) )

      end   as Month_Year
}

2. Meta data Extension:

 

Code:

@Metadata.layer: #CORE

@UI.chart: [{
    qualifier: 'chartDefault',
    description: 'Purchase Data',
    chartType: #PIE,
    dimensions: ['Month_Year'],
    measures: ['Amount'],
    dimensionAttributes: [{ dimension: 'Month_Year',role: #SERIES }],
    measureAttributes: [{ measure: 'Amount', role: #AXIS_1 }]
    }
   ]

@UI.presentationVariant: [{
    qualifier: 'Default',
    visualizations: [{ type: #AS_CHART ,qualifier: 'chartDefault'}]
   
     
}]



annotate entity Ztest_ALP_Report with
{
  .lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 10 }]

  SalesDocument;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 20 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 20 }]
  SalesOrg;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 30 }]
  Plant;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 40 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 40 }]
  DistrChannel;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 50 }]
  .dataPoint.qualifier: 'Amount'
  Amount;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 60 }]
  Currency;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 70 }]
  D_Date;
  .lineItem: [{ position: 80 }]
  .selectionField: [{ position: 80 }]
  Month_Year;

}

 

Step 2: Consume the CDS View in an OData Service

Note: It is assumed that you already know how to consume a CDS View in an OData service. Therefore, this article does not cover the step-by-step process. Instead, the explanation below directly continues with the required configuration and examples.

Go to SEGW Tcode and open your Project and   Add Your CDS View.

After adding the CDS entity, the Entity Type, Entity Set, and related properties will be generated automatically in the project.

Step 3:  Create SAP Fiori Application using Analytical List Page Template

Follow the steps below:

Open BAS  and Click  on File  and Select as New Project from Template  Chose  SAP Fiori  Generator Template under the Template Select as Analytical List Page and  Provide  Required Details and Finish it.

After completing these steps, the system automatically generates the SAP Fiori Elements Analytical List Page  application.

 

Once all the above steps are completed and the configuration is finished, preview the application in SAP Business Application Studio. The application will load successfully, and you will be able to see the Analytical List Page with the data displayed in charts and tables.

In the CDS annotations, I have configured the chart type as a Pie Chart by default. For this reason, the data is displayed as a Pie Chart in the above screenshot. However, the chart type can be changed based on the client's business requirements.
For Your Reference: This annotation has already been added in the Metadata Extension View.

Depending on the client’s requirements, you can change the chart type in the Fiori Elements application to formats such as Pie Chart, Column Chart, Bar Chart, or Line Chart. Please see the screenshot below for reference.

 

 

Conclusion: In conclusion, the Analytical List Page in SAP Fiori Elements provides an effective way to analyze and monitor business data through interactive charts, visual filters, and tables. By combining data visualization with filtering capabilities, it helps users gain insights quickly and make informed decisions more efficiently.

 

Please go below Links:

 Visual Filter: https://community.sap.com/t5/abap-blog-posts/kpi-key-performance-indicator-a-simple-guide/ba-p/14348850 

KPI(Key Performance Indicator): //community.sap.com/t5/abap-blog-posts/visual-filters-in-sap-fiori-elements-a-complete-guide/ba-p/14348265 

 

Thank You,

B Naresh Kumar Nayak

entry.title: Re: Avoiding Double Counting of Yield/Scrap in Rework Sce...
entry.content:
Hi @okakoshow 

SAP Digital Manufacturing (DM) sends yield/scrap confirmations to SAP ERP/SAP S/4HANA whenever an SFC completes an operation that is marked as an ERP reporting step. DM also allows an SFC to be re-processed at an operation it has already completed. In such a case, completing the same ERP reporting step again will trigger an additional confirmation, and the integrated ERP system will accumulate those quantities as you describe. The Production Yield Confirmation message no longer carries a “final confirmation” flag, so ERP sets the operation to PCNF and continues to sum posted quantities; CNF is only set after the order is completed in DM. There is no standard netting or de-duplication mechanism described that would automatically prevent double counting when the same reporting step is confirmed twice.

This is the recommended approach to avoid double counting.

  • Use a single ERP reporting step at the end of your routings:

    • The documentation recommends marking the last routing operation as the ERP reporting step to ensure correct reporting of consumed components and confirmations. With this setup, yield is only communicated when the SFC completes the final (reporting) operation, so sending the SFC back to earlier operations for rework will not trigger additional yield postings.
    • For planned orders, yield confirmation is explicitly sent after the completion of the last operation; aligning production orders with this pattern helps avoid duplicates.

  • Design rework as separate, non-reporting steps:

    • DM supports rework loops (for example TEST/DEBUG/REPAIR) within the main routing. Configure these rework operations without the ERP reporting flag so that rework does not generate additional yield confirmations.
    • If an SFC is scrapped on a non-reporting step, DM will automatically find a future reporting step in the routing and use it to send the scrap confirmation. This ensures scrap is reported once without relying on early, repeatable reporting steps.

Hope this helps.

Best,

Mike

entry.title: Re: Week 3 : Use RAP Business Logic Prediction to Implement Validation
entry.content:
Week 3: Challenge Completed. Package ZAC_16246

Auto generated code:

METHOD ValidateCategory.

    READ ENTITIES OF zr_personalexpensetracker IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY PersonalExpenseTracker
        FIELDS ( PaymentMethod )
        WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
      RESULT DATA(entities).

    LOOP AT entities INTO DATA(entity).
      " Validate if PaymentMethod is not within the allowed values
      IF entity-PaymentMethod <> 'EMI' AND
         entity-PaymentMethod <> 'CARD' AND
         entity-PaymentMethod <> 'CASH'.
        APPEND VALUE #( %tky = entity-%tky ) TO failed-PersonalExpenseTracker.
        APPEND VALUE #(
            %tky                = entity-%tky
            %state_area         = 'PaymentMethod'
            %msg                = new_message_with_text(
                                    text     = 'Invalid Payment Method. Allowed values are EMI, CARD, CASH.'
                                    severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
                                  )
        ) TO reported-PersonalExpenseTracker.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: Re: CBC behavior when an activated scope item is deprecat...
entry.content:
Hello @UmenoYagi,

the following two links can be helpful regarding scope item deactivation:

  • 3649879 - Deprecated Scope Items - SAP Central Business Configuration - SAP for Me
  • Deprecated Scope Items | SAP Help Portal

 

To apply the info from both links to your questions:

 

  • Will the successor scope item be automatically activated during the upgrade? 

    → No, SAP specifically recomends "to opt for the successor scope items (when available)" in the above mentioned KBA. Therefore, activation of the successor scope item is a manual task by the customer or implementation team.

 

  • Will it be automatically applied or transportedto the customer systems (e.g. test / production)?

    → No, a manual transport is necessary. After saving the scope change and deploying the scope and organizational structure in CBC a transport will be created that needs to be transported manually.

 

  • What happens to the original scope item that is deprecated or removed?
    • Is it automatically deactivated or deleted during the upgrade?

      → Here i can not give you a specific answer but an example from our set-up: Scope Item 1QR was deprecated with Release 2602:

      During our 2602 preparations we performed a check and left some notes. As of today, 1QR still appears in CBC under "My Selected Scope". So it seems that is has not been removed yet. 

       

I hope this helps.

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: Re: Business AI Makes the Data Layer More Important, Not Less
entry.content: I may be wrong but I don't think companies choose SAP BDC etc for it's qualities they choose SAP BDC because they use SAP as ERP system. At the same time SAP does a lot to protect SAPs rights to be the only ones that can access the data in the system. Would SAP AI and BDC be the first choice of company that don't run SAP ERP? The only differentiator here is access to data. I will argue that SAP is preventing competition in the area of business AI, prove me wrong, please!
